notes: Lacedaemonian military figure and governor in Thespiae, known for boldness and involvement in factional conflicts during the 4th century BCE.

docs.title: Life of Agesilaus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … was governor in Thespiae, a bold and enterprising man, though he had perhaps more of confidence than wisdom. This action of Phoebidas fired him, and incited his ambition to attempt some great enterprise, which might render him as famous as he perceived the taking of the Cadmea had

docs.title: Life of Pelopidas
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … full of ungrounded hopes and foolish ambition, was left with an army at Thespiae, to receive and succor the Theban renegades. To him Pelopidas and his colleagues privately sent a merchant, one of their friends, with money, and, what proved more efficient, advice, — that it more became
